title: The Docket - Week 2
subject: OpenAI just hired 50 lawyers. Not to defend themselves.
preheader: The legal AI arms race enters a new phase, plus a clause-checking trick you'll use today
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Week of April 1, 2026

No, it’s not an April Fools’ joke. Here’s what actually happened this week.

🎯 OpenAI’s Legal Products Division Is Real

OpenAI posted 50+ legal domain roles last week — not for their legal department, but for a new “Legal Products” division. They’re building vertical solutions, not just APIs. This changes the competitive landscape for every legal AI startup relying on OpenAI’s models. When your platform becomes your competitor, the playbook needs rewriting. See our current AI tool rankings.

💰 Legal AI Funding Hits Q1 Record

$1.8B in legal AI funding closed in Q1 2026, more than all of 2024 combined. The biggest round: $340M for a contract lifecycle management platform that claims to reduce outside counsel spend by 30%. Investors are betting that legal is the next vertical to fully transform. Skeptics say we’ve heard this before.

🇪🇺 EU AI Act Enforcement Begins Biting

First enforcement actions under the EU AI Act are targeting legal tech providers who classify their tools as “low risk” when they’re used in judicial decision-making contexts. If your tool helps decide case outcomes, you’re high-risk. Period. Several vendors are scrambling to reclassify.

Tool Spotlight

Clause Checker

Paste any contract clause and get instant analysis: ambiguity flags, missing standard protections, unusual language, and risk scoring. It’s the second pair of eyes that doesn’t bill by the hour.

Check a Clause Free →

See you next Wednesday. Hit reply if something in here made you think — or made you angry. Both work.
